new.title: GitHub issues
new.text: See and create GitHub issues right next to your Linear ones. Connect GitHub in Settings → Accounts with a secure device sign-in — no passwords or tokens to paste — pick the repositories you care about, and flip between the Linear and GitHub tabs in the menu.

new.title: Full-screen meeting alerts
new.text: A calm full-screen alert with a live countdown appears when a meeting starts — join with one click or dismiss with Esc. Turn it on and set how early it shows in Settings → Calendar.

new.title: Issue filters
new.text: Choose per provider whether the menu shows only issues assigned to you or every open issue across your selected Linear teams and GitHub repositories, in Settings → Issues.

new.title: Issue actions
new.text: Hover any Linear or GitHub issue in the menu and press a hotkey to change its status, priority, due date, labels, or assignees. Pick your keys in Settings → Shortcuts.

new.title: GitHub issue editor
new.text: Press ⌃⌥⌘G from anywhere to file a GitHub issue with repository, assignee, and label pickers. Set a default repository in Settings → Issues.

new.title: Settings, redesigned
new.text: A System Settings-style window with a sidebar and search across every option — General, Accounts, Calendar, Issues, Notes, and Shortcuts. Dayline now shows a Dock icon while any of its windows is open.

new.title: Menu sections
new.text: Hide the calendar, issues, or notes sections from the menu with the “Show … in menu” toggles in Settings.

new.title: Public changelog
new.text: Every release, explained, at dayline.robin.build/changelog — also linked from Settings → About.

fixed.text: The Linear issue creator now offers the states, estimates, projects, and labels of the team you picked.
Behind the scenes: release-pipeline hardening, repository security defaults, and automated code review.

new.title: Update controls
new.text: Settings → About now shows your exact version and build, a Check for Updates button, and an “Install updates automatically” toggle so Dayline can quietly keep itself current.

fixed.text: Hardened the release pipeline and protected automatic update publishing.

new.title: Global shortcuts
new.text: Press ⌃⌥⌘N for a new note or ⌃⌥⌘L for a new Linear issue — from anywhere, even when Dayline is in the background. Record your own in Settings → Shortcuts.

new.title: Linear issue editor
new.text: Rebuilt with colored pickers for state, priority, assignee, and labels, plus a graphical due-date picker.

new.title: Due dates
new.text: Hover a Linear issue and press the hotkey to set or clear its due date from a native calendar popover.

new.title: Copy actions
new.text: Copy a hovered issue’s link or its suggested git branch name with one hotkey — pick your favorite in Settings.

new.title: Creation defaults
new.text: New Linear issues start with your preferred team, state, assignee, and labels already filled in.

new.title: Anonymous feedback
new.text: Submit feedback right from the app — it lands as a public GitHub issue, no account needed.

fixed.text: Linear issue lists now load in pages, so long backlogs stay fast.
Refreshed the menu header with the Dayline wordmark and a new website social preview.
Behind the scenes: releases are now resumable and much more reliable.

new.title: Automatic updates
new.text: Dayline checks for new versions and installs them for you — no more re-downloading from the website.

fixed.text: Removed the status menu’s scroll indicator for a cleaner look.

new.title: Multiple Google accounts
new.text: Connect more than one Google account and pick calendars per account — Settings → Accounts keeps them side by side.

new.title: Dayline on the web
new.text: dayline.robin.build is live, with downloads, the Homebrew command, and the legal bits.

new.title: Direct sign-in
new.text: Connect Google and Linear inside the app with secure OAuth — no gcloud or Linear CLI installs required anymore.

new.title: Current meeting in the menu bar
new.text: The meeting you’re in stays pinned next to the clock until it ends — so you always know what’s now, not just what’s next.

new.title: Quick notes
new.text: Jot things down without leaving the menu — create, edit, reorder, and delete notes, with the first line as the title. Everything is stored only on your Mac.

new.title: Linear issues from the menu
new.text: Create issues with team and assignee pickers, change states, bump priority, or swipe a row to cancel an issue — no browser detour.

new.title: Now-playing highlight
new.text: The event happening right now gets a subtle green highlight, so it stands out from what’s next.

fixed.text: Fixed Google and Linear sign-in detection and added a Launch at Login setting.

new.title: First public release
new.text: Today’s Google Calendar agenda, your active Linear issues, and what’s next — all in one quiet menu bar app.
